summary: Iteration 80 - Testing new Audit Logs system for invoice and table (bon) modifications. Backend: 22/24 tests passed (92%). Frontend: All UI elements verified - Audit tab visible only for Admin, filters, KPIs, log rows, detail modal all working correctly.

critical.issue: Table update does NOT create audit log. The tables.py router with audit logging exists but is NOT included in server.py. Server.py has duplicate table endpoints without audit logging.
Table deletion with reason=cancelled does NOT create audit log. Same root cause - tables.py router not included in server.py.

action_items: CRITICAL: Include tables.py router in server.py OR add audit logging to the table endpoints in server.py. Currently tables.py has _log_audit calls but server.py table endpoints do not.
The tables.py router (lines 90-192) has proper audit logging for PUT and DELETE with reason=cancelled, but server.py (lines 4064-4115) has duplicate endpoints without audit logging.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend invoices.py: Audit logging working correctly for all invoice operations (create, update, validate, cancel, delete, update-items)
Backend invoices.py: _log_audit function properly excludes MongoDB _id from responses
Backend invoices.py: _diff_invoice function correctly computes field-level diffs with items summary
Backend invoices.py: GET /api/audit/logs correctly restricts access to admin role only (403 for manager/server)
Backend tables.py: Has proper audit logging implementation but router is NOT included in server.py
Backend server.py: Has duplicate table endpoints (lines 4064-4115) that do NOT call _log_audit
Frontend AuditLogsTab.jsx: Well-implemented with all required data-testid attributes
Frontend CaissePage.jsx: Correctly shows Audit tab only for admin role (line 5038-5044)
seed_data_creation: Test invoices and tables created and cleaned up during tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 80 tested new Audit Logs system. Invoice audit logging works perfectly (create, validate, cancel, delete, update-items). Table audit logging DOES NOT WORK because tables.py router is not included in server.py - the server.py has duplicate table endpoints without audit calls. Key data-testids: tab-audit, audit-logs-tab, audit-search-input, audit-entity-filter, audit-actor-filter, audit-action-filter, audit-refresh-btn, audit-date-from, audit-date-to, audit-row-{id}, audit-detail-{id}. Backend endpoint: GET /api/audit/logs?role=admin returns {total, by_action, by_actor, logs}. Returns 403 for non-admin roles.

backend: GET /api/audit/logs without role returns 422 (required param) - PASSED
POST /api/invoices creates audit log with action='create' - PASSED
PUT /api/invoices/{id} with validation_status='validated' creates log action='validate' with diff - PASSED
PUT /api/invoices/{id} with validation_status='cancelled' creates log action='cancel' with diff - PASSED
PUT /api/invoices/{id}/update-items creates log with items diff - PASSED
PUT /api/caisse/tables/{id} creates audit log - FAILED (tables.py router not included)
DELETE /api/caisse/tables/{id}?reason=cancelled creates audit log - FAILED (tables.py router not included)
DELETE /api/caisse/tables/{id} without reason does NOT create log - PASSED
Audit log structure has required fields - PASSED
frontend: Audit tab visible for Admin (data-testid='tab-audit') - PASSED
Audit tab NOT visible for Gérante (Manager) - PASSED
Audit log rows displayed (17 rows found) - PASSED
Detail modal shows event info correctly - PASSED
KPIs displayed: Total, Création, Modification, Validation, Annulation, Suppression - PASSED

root_cause_analysis.root_cause: The tables.py router file exists with proper _log_audit calls but is NOT included in server.py via include_router(). Instead, server.py has duplicate table endpoints (lines 4064-4115) that do not call _log_audit.
evidence: tables.py lines 90-192: Has PUT and DELETE endpoints with _log_audit calls
server.py lines 4064-4115: Has duplicate PUT and DELETE endpoints WITHOUT _log_audit calls
server.py lines 110-128: include_router() calls do NOT include tables_router
Test results: 0 table audit logs created despite table operations being performed
fix_options: Option 1: Add 'from routers.tables import router as tables_router, set_db as set_tables_db' and 'api_router.include_router(tables_router)' to server.py, then remove duplicate endpoints
Option 2: Add _log_audit calls to the existing table endpoints in server.py (lines 4064-4115)
